inputs.label: Cost of Goods Sold (Annual)
inputs.businessContext: Annual cost of goods sold. Standard financial metric per GAAP/IFRS.

inputs.label: Average Inventory Value
inputs.businessContext: Average inventory over the period. Calculated as (beginning inventory + ending inventory)/2. Per accounting standards.

inputs.label: Days in Period
inputs.businessContext: Number of days in the analysis period. Typically 365 for annual.

inputs.label: Industry Benchmark Turnover
inputs.businessContext: Average inventory turnover ratio for the retail sector. Source: WERC/APICS benchmarks.

inputs.label: Demand Variability (Coefficient of Variation)
inputs.businessContext: Coefficient of variation of demand. Higher values indicate higher risk. Based on supply chain risk management literature.

inputs.label: Lead Time (Days)
inputs.businessContext: Average replenishment lead time from suppliers. Key input for safety stock calculations.

inputs.label: Service Level Target
inputs.businessContext: Desired cycle service level. Common values: 95% for standard, 97.5% for premium. Based on inventory theory.

inputs.label: Data Confidence Score
inputs.businessContext: Confidence in input data accuracy. Adjusts final risk score. Common in risk assessment frameworks.

hiddenLossDrivers: if inventoryTurnoverRatio < 2 then 'Low turnover indicates overstocking or obsolescence risk'
if demandVariability > 0.5 then 'High demand variability increases safety stock and risk'
if leadTimeDays > 60 then 'Long lead time increases supply chain vulnerability'
suggestedActions: if inventoryTurnoverRatio < industryBenchmarkTurnover then 'Review slow-moving items and consider markdowns or liquidation'
if demandVariability > 0.5 then 'Implement demand forecasting improvements and safety stock optimization'
if leadTimeDays > 60 then 'Explore supplier diversification or nearshoring to reduce lead time'
General: 'Conduct ABC analysis to prioritize inventory management efforts'

premiumFeatures: Detailed Report with breakdowns and charts
